design:
Goal
Track a conservative simplification pass over duplicated logic identified in the March 2026 audit. The work should reduce maintenance bloat and repeated control flow without changing user-facing behavior.

Scope
This epic covers the independently actionable refactor categories discovered in cmd/roborev, cmd/roborev/tui, internal/agent, and internal/daemon.

Non-goals
- No feature changes
- No behavior changes to CLI/TUI output beyond bug-for-bug preservation
- No broad rewrites without tests anchoring current behavior

Success Criteria
- Each child issue has a narrow, behavior-preserving refactor scope
- The highest-value duplicate code paths are tracked separately so they can be executed incrementally
- Each child issue names concrete files, edge cases, and acceptance criteria
- Each completed child issue explicitly includes running /roborev:fix after implementation before closure
title: Simplification pass: code reuse and deduplication

design:
Goal
Eliminate overlapping job polling and review-fetch logic across the CLI and daemon HTTP client layers while preserving current command-specific output and exit-code behavior.

Target Files
- cmd/roborev/daemon_client.go
- cmd/roborev/job_helpers.go
- cmd/roborev/run.go
- cmd/roborev/analyze.go
- internal/daemon/client.go

Change
- Identify the canonical polling/fetch abstraction for job status and final review retrieval.
- Collapse duplicate waitFor* implementations where the only differences are output formatting or verdict handling.
- Leave intentionally distinct flows separate when they differ materially, but route them through shared status/review helpers.

Edge Cases
- Context cancellation and timeout handling
- Unknown future job statuses
- done status arriving before review becomes readable
- Prompt jobs that should not use verdict-based exit codes
- Quiet mode versus streaming output mode

Acceptance Criteria
- Independent job polling implementations are reduced to a small deliberate set rather than separate ad hoc loops per command
- cmd/roborev/daemon_client.go no longer duplicates review/status fetch logic already represented in internal/daemon/client.go or a new shared helper
- Review, run, analyze, and compact flows preserve current output and exit-code semantics under targeted tests
- No daemon/background behavior change is introduced while deduplicating the client-side logic
- After implementation is complete, /roborev:fix has been run before the issue is considered done

Anti-patterns
- Do not collapse materially different UX flows into one unreadable mega-function
- Do not change retry/backoff semantics unless tests prove the old behavior was accidental
title: Unify daemon review polling and CLI daemon client reuse

design:
Goal
Reduce repeated transport and loader boilerplate in the TUI without obscuring Bubble Tea state transitions.

Target Files
- cmd/roborev/tui/api.go
- cmd/roborev/tui/fetch.go
- cmd/roborev/tui/actions.go
- cmd/roborev/tui/handlers_msg.go

Change
- Standardize the TUI around a small set of synchronous load helpers such as loadReview, loadJob, loadPatch, paged job loaders, and repo/branch loaders plus thin tea.Cmd wrappers.
- Reuse getJSON/postJSON consistently rather than open-coding request/response handling.
- Keep message construction local to the command wrappers so update flows remain explicit.

Edge Cases
- 404 versus generic server failures
- Clipboard/copy flows that need loaded job metadata
- Pagination and branch filter sentinels
- Reconnect and stale fetch sequencing

Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated GET/POST request plus decode boilerplate in fetch.go and actions.go is routed through a shared loader layer
- fetchReviewAndCopy, fetchPatchAndJob, fetchJobByID, and similar functions reuse shared synchronous loaders instead of each issuing bespoke transport logic
- Bubble Tea message types and optimistic-update behavior remain explicit and current TUI fetch/review/copy tests continue to pass
- Transport helpers stay simple enough that a maintainer can still follow each async state transition from dispatch to message handling
- After implementation is complete, /roborev:fix has been run before the issue is considered done

Anti-patterns
- Do not introduce generic reflection-heavy request helpers
- Do not hide message typing behind callbacks that make update flow harder to follow
title: Normalize TUI HTTP loading and Tea command wrappers

design:
Goal
Remove repetitive field-copy boilerplate from agent WithReasoning, WithAgentic, WithModel, and WithSessionID methods while preserving immutable-style behavior.

Target Files
- internal/agent/codex.go
- internal/agent/claude.go
- internal/agent/gemini.go
- internal/agent/cursor.go
- internal/agent/copilot.go
- internal/agent/opencode.go
- internal/agent/pi.go
- internal/agent/kilo.go
- internal/agent/kiro.go
- internal/agent/droid.go

Change
- Introduce a small internal helper or embedded config pattern for the common command/model/reasoning/agentic/session state each agent clones.
- Keep type-specific fields and behavior local to each agent.
- Preserve the current contract that empty model inputs keep the existing/default model where appropriate.

Edge Cases
- Agents with session state versus agents without it
- Agents where WithModel("") intentionally returns the existing instance
- Agents with extra fields such as provider or built-in defaults

Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated copy constructors across agent adapters are substantially reduced through a shared internal pattern
- Public agent behavior, including default-model preservation and session sanitization, remains unchanged
- Existing agent tests covering reasoning/model/session persistence continue to pass or are expanded to lock the preserved behavior
- The resulting abstraction remains internal and obvious rather than introducing a second parallel agent hierarchy
- After implementation is complete, /roborev:fix has been run before the issue is considered done

Anti-patterns
- Do not erase agent-specific semantics just to force uniformity
- Do not add exported compatibility wrappers for old construction paths
title: Extract shared agent configuration cloning helpers

design:
Goal
Make workflow-based agent selection use one shared precedence path across CLI, daemon, and batch-review callers.

Target Files
- cmd/roborev/fix.go
- cmd/roborev/refine.go
- internal/agent/model_resolution.go
- internal/daemon/worker.go
- internal/review/batch.go

Change
- Extract a shared workflow-resolution helper that determines preferred agent, backup selection, resolved model, and reasoning context.
- Remove duplicated preferred-versus-backup comparison code from fix/refine/worker paths.
- Keep caller-specific behavior separate, for example agentic mode, retry policy, and provider configuration.

Edge Cases
- Backup agent selected via alias/canonical name mismatch
- Empty CLI model meaning preserve agent default unless workflow/backup config overrides it
- Fix workflow versus review/security/design workflow mapping
- Daemon failover behavior versus foreground CLI selection

Acceptance Criteria
- Fix, refine, daemon worker, and batch review paths use the same workflow precedence rules for primary agent, backup agent, and model selection
- Duplicated preferred-versus-backup comparison logic is removed from at least the currently duplicated CLI/daemon call sites
- Existing tests around backup model selection and workflow model resolution continue to pass, with new coverage added where current behavior was only implicit
- Caller-specific concerns such as retry policy and WithAgentic(true) remain outside the shared resolver
- After implementation is complete, /roborev:fix has been run before the issue is considered done

Anti-patterns
- Do not mix config resolution with execution/retry orchestration in one helper
- Do not change fallback order without explicit tests proving intent
title: Unify workflow agent, model, and backup resolution

design:
Goal
Ensure each agent adapter has one source of truth for CLI argument assembly, avoiding drift between CommandLine previews and actual runtime args.

Target Files
- internal/agent/cursor.go
- internal/agent/pi.go
- internal/agent/codex.go
- internal/agent/claude.go
- internal/agent/gemini.go
- internal/agent/copilot.go
- internal/agent/opencode.go
- internal/agent/kilo.go
- internal/agent/kiro.go
- internal/agent/droid.go

Change
- Refactor CommandLine to derive from a single argument-construction path per agent, with an explicit preview/runtime distinction only where necessary.
- Remove duplicated inline argument assembly that repeats buildArgs behavior.
- Preserve omission of runtime-only values like prompt stdin and repo path when rendering previews.

Edge Cases
- Session resume arguments that are valid for execution but should be sanitized for preview
- Agents whose preview intentionally substitutes a default model like auto
- Runtime-only arguments such as repo path, stdin markers, or temp prompt files

Acceptance Criteria
- Each agent adapter has one obvious argument builder rather than separate partially duplicated preview and runtime code paths
- CommandLine still emits a representative preview without leaking runtime-only prompt or path values
- Existing build-arg and command-line tests continue to pass and cover any preview/runtime split that remains necessary
- No agent changes its actual executed flags as an accidental side effect of the cleanup
- After implementation is complete, /roborev:fix has been run before the issue is considered done

Anti-patterns
- Do not force all agents through one global builder if their CLIs materially differ
- Do not silently change preview output formatting without updating tests intentionally
title: Unify agent command preview and argument builders

design:
Goal
Reduce repeated flag/repo/global/default precedence logic across CI-related commands and workflow-file generation.

Target Files
- cmd/roborev/ci.go
- cmd/roborev/ghaction.go
- internal/config/config.go only if a shared helper belongs there

Change
- Consolidate the repeated precedence patterns used by resolveAgentList, resolveReviewTypes, resolveCIReasoning, resolveCIMinSeverity, resolveCISynthesisAgent, resolveCIUpsertComments, and resolveWorkflowConfig.
- Keep command-specific UX and output separate from shared resolution logic.
- Prefer a small shared config-resolver helper over open-coded precedence ladders in multiple command files.

Edge Cases
- Empty flag meaning auto-detect rather than explicit empty value
- Repo config overriding global config only for some fields
- Workflow generation needing a list while CLI review execution may accept an empty-string sentinel

Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated flag > repo > global > default precedence code is consolidated into a shared helper or small helper family
- ci.go and ghaction.go derive agent/workflow configuration consistently from the same precedence rules
- Existing CI-related tests continue to pass, with added coverage where precedence behavior was previously duplicated but unpinned
- The extracted helper remains simple and data-focused rather than embedding command execution concerns
- After implementation is complete, /roborev:fix has been run before the issue is considered done

Anti-patterns
- Do not push command-specific side effects into shared config helpers
- Do not replace explicit precedence with reflection or map-based indirection that hides behavior
title: Consolidate CI and workflow config precedence helpers

design:
Goal
Remove repeated repo-root, main-repo-root, and current-branch discovery snippets in fix-related command paths.

Target Files
- cmd/roborev/fix.go
- Related callers in cmd/roborev/compact.go or adjacent command files only if they can share the same helper cleanly

Change
- Extract helpers for the common patterns currently repeated in fix list/open/batch/single-job flows: working directory lookup, worktree root resolution, main repo root resolution for daemon/API queries, and current branch fallback when --branch is omitted.
- Use those helpers consistently so fix code talks about intent instead of path plumbing.

Edge Cases
- Git worktrees versus main repo root
- Detached HEAD or missing current branch
- Commands that query the daemon using main root but operate on the current worktree root

Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated os.Getwd plus git.GetRepoRoot plus git.GetMainRepoRoot plus git.GetCurrentBranch snippets in fix flows are replaced by shared helpers
- Worktree-root versus main-repo-root behavior remains unchanged for daemon queries and local file operations
- Fix list/open/batch/single-job paths read more directly in terms of intent after extraction
- Existing fix tests continue to pass, with targeted additions for worktree/main-root behavior if current coverage is thin
- After implementation is complete, /roborev:fix has been run before the issue is considered done

Anti-patterns
- Do not hide unrelated fix orchestration in the new helper
- Do not normalize away meaningful distinctions between worktree root and main repo root
title: Extract shared repo-root and branch resolution helpers for fix flows
